title: Plain-language mode for constituents + officials
context: Statutory language is often dense and jargon-heavy. A plain-language mode that re-renders duty descriptions, ethics rules, and casework status in plain English (at a defined reading level) would benefit both officials absorbing their duties and constituents understanding their case status. Plain-language output remains informational-not-legal-advice and always cites the underlying primary source. This is a readability and literacy benefit.
objective: Implement a plain-language mode that re-renders duty/ethics content and casework status in plain English at an accessible reading level, while preserving the underlying primary-source citations and informational-not-legal-advice labeling.
acceptanceCriteria: Plain-language mode available as a toggle on duty/ethics content and casework status displays
Plain-language output targets a defined readability level (e.g., Flesch-Kincaid grade 8 or below)
Underlying primary-source citation is always preserved and displayed alongside the plain-language text
Informational-not-legal-advice label is preserved in plain-language mode
output: A merged PR adding a plain-language toggle to duty/ethics content and casework status displays, preserving source citations and informational labeling.
